sections.label: Section 3.3.556
sections.original: Noli metuere : una tecum bona mala tolerabimus .
sections.translation: Don ’ t be afraid : together with you , we ’ ll share good and bad .

sections.label: Section 3.3.557
sections.original: Quantum tibi opus est argenti ? loquere .
sections.translation: How much money do you want ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.557b
sections.translation: Only thirty minae .

sections.label: Section 3.3.558
sections.translation: Thirty ? Heyday ! she ’ s monstrous dear , Phaedria .

sections.label: Section 3.3.558b
sections.translation: Indeed , she ’ s very cheap .

sections.label: Section 3.3.559
sections.translation: Well , well , I ’ ll get them for you .

sections.label: Section 3.3.559b
sections.translation: Oh the dear man ! ( They both fall to hugging GETA .)

sections.label: Section 3.3.559c
sections.translation: Take yourselves off . ( Shakes them of .)

sections.label: Section 3.3.559d
sections.translation: There ’ s need for them directly .

sections.label: Section 3.3.559e
sections.translation: You shall have them directly ;

sections.label: Section 3.3.560
sections.original: Sed opus est mihi Phormionem ad hanc rem adiutorem dari .
sections.translation: but I must have Phormio for my assistant in this business .

sections.label: Section 3.3.561
sections.original: Praesto est : audacissime oneris quidvis impone , et feret .
sections.translation: He ’ s quite ready ; right boldly lay on him any load you like , he ’ ll bear it : he , in especial , is a friend to his friend .

sections.label: Section 3.3.562b
sections.translation: Let ’ s go to him at once then .

sections.label: Section 3.3.563
sections.original: Numquid est quod opera mea vobis opus sit ?
sections.translation: Will you have any occasion for my assistance ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.563b
sections.translation: None ; but be off home , and comfort that poor thing , who I am sure is now in-doors almost dead with fear . Do you linger ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.565b
sections.original: Nihil est aeque quod faciam libens .
sections.translation: There ’ s nothing I could do with so much pleasure .

sections.label: Section 3.3.566
sections.translation: What way will you manage this ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.566b
sections.original: Dicam in itinere : modo te hinc amove .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you on the road ; first thing , betake yourself off . ( Exeunt .)

sections.label: Section 4.1.567
sections.original: Quid qua profectus causa hinc es Lemnum , Chreme ,
sections.translation: Well , have you brought your daughter with you , Chremes , for whom you went to Lemnos ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.568b
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 4.1.568c
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.569
sections.original: Postquam videt me eius mater esse hic diutius ,
sections.translation: When her mother found that I staid here longer than usual ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.570
sections.original: Simul autem non manebat aetas virginis
sections.translation: and at the same time the age of the girl did not suit with my delays , they told me that she , with all her family , set out in search of me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.572b
sections.translation: Pray , then , why did you stay there so long , when you had heard of this ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.574
sections.translation: Why , faith , a malady detained me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.574b
sections.translation: From what cause ? Or what was it ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.574c
sections.translation: Do you ask me ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.575
sections.original: Senectus ipsa est morbus ; sed venisse eas
sections.translation: Old age itself is a malady . However , I heard that they had arrived safe , from the caps tain who brought them .

sections.label: Section 4.1.577
sections.original: Quid gnato obtigerit me absente audistin , Chreme ?
sections.translation: Have you heard , Chremes , what has happened to my son in my absence ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.578
sections.original: Quod quidem me factum consili incertum facit ;
sections.translation: ’ Tis that , in fact , that has embarrassed me in my plans . For if I offer my daughter in marriage to any person that ’ s a stranger ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.580
sections.original: Quo pacto aut unde mihi sit dicendum ordine est .
sections.translation: it must all be told how and by whom I had her . You I knew to be fully as faithful to me as I am to myself ; if a stranger shall think fit to be connected with me by marriage , he will hold his tongue , just as long as good terms exist between us : but if he takes a dislike to me , he ’ ll be knowing more than it ’ s proper he should know .

sections.label: Section 4.1.585
sections.original: Vereorque ne uxor aliqua hoc resciscat mea ;
sections.translation: I am afraid , too , lest my wife should , by some means , come to know of it ; if that is the case , it only remains for me to shake myself [*] and leave the house ; for I ’ m the only one I can rely on at home . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.1.588
sections.original: Scio ita esse ; et istaec mihi res sollicitudini est :
sections.translation: I know it is so , and that circumstance is a cause of anxiety to me ; and I shall never cease trying ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.590
sections.original: Donec tibi id quod pollicitus sum effecero .
sections.translation: until I ’ ve made good what I promised you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.591
sections.translation: I never saw a more cunning fellow than this Phormio . I came to the fellow to tell him that money was needed , and by what means it might be procured . Hardly had I said one half , when he understood me ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.595
sections.translation: he was quite delighted ; complimented me ; asked where the old man was ; gave thanks to the Gods that an opportunity was afforded him for showing himself no less a friend to Phaedria than to Antipho : I bade the fellow wait for me at the Forum ; whither I would bring the old gentleman .

sections.label: Section 4.2.600
sections.original: Sed eccum ipsum : quis est ulterior ? atat , Phaedriae
sections.translation: But see , here ’ s the very man ( catching sight of the Old Man ) . Who is the further one ? Heyday , Phaedria ’ s father has got back ! still , brute beast that I am , what was I afraid of ? Is it because two are presented instead of one for me to dupe ? I deem it preferable to enjoy a two-fold hope . I ’ ll try for it from him from whom I first intended : if he gives it me , well and good ; if I can make nothing of him , then I ’ ll attack this new-comer .

sections.label: Section 4.3.606
sections.original: Exspecto quam mox recipiat huc sese Geta :
sections.translation: I ’ m expecting every moment that Geta will be here . But I see my uncle standing close by , with my father . Ah me ! how much I fear what influence his return may have upon my father !

sections.label: Section 4.3.609
sections.translation: I ’ ll accost them . ( Goes up to them .) O welcome to you , our neighbor Chremes .

sections.label: Section 4.3.609b
sections.translation: Save you , Geta .

sections.label: Section 4.3.610
sections.translation: I ’ m delighted to see you safe returned .

sections.label: Section 4.3.610b
sections.translation: I believe you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.610c
sections.translation: How go matters ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.611
sections.original: Multa advenienti , ut fit , nova hic compluria .
sections.translation: Many changes here upon my arrival , as usually the case .

sections.label: Section 4.3.612
sections.original: Ita : de Antiphone audistin quae facta ?
sections.translation: True ; have you heard what has happened to Antipho ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.612b
sections.translation: All .

sections.label: Section 4.3.613
sections.original: Tun dixeras huic ? facinus indignum , Chreme ,
sections.translation: What , have you told him ? Disgraceful conduct , Chremes , thus to be imposed on .

sections.label: Section 4.3.614b
sections.translation: It was about that I was talking to him just now .

sections.label: Section 4.3.615
sections.original: Nalm hercle ego quoque id quidem agitans mecum sedulo
sections.translation: But really , on carefully reflecting upon this matter I think I have found a remedy .

sections.label: Section 4.3.617
sections.translation: What is the remedy ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.617b
sections.original: Ut abii abs te , fit forte obviam
sections.translation: When I left you , by accident Phormio met me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.618b
sections.translation: Who is Phormio ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.618c
sections.translation: He who patronized her .

sections.label: Section 4.3.618d
sections.translation: I understand .

sections.label: Section 4.3.619
sections.original: Visum est mihi ut eius tentarem sententiam .
sections.translation: It seemed to me that I might first sound him ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.620
sections.original: Prendo hominem solum : Cur non , inquam , Phormio ,
sections.translation: I took the fellow aside : Phormio , said I , why don ’ t we try to settle these matters between us rather with a good grace than with a bad one ? My master ’ s a generous man , and one who hates litigation ; but really , upon my faith , all his friends were just now

sections.label: Section 4.3.625
sections.original: Uno ore auctores fuere ut praecipitem hanc daret .
sections.translation: advising him with one voice to turn her instantly out of doors .

sections.label: Section 4.3.626
sections.original: Quid hic coeptat ? aut quo evadet hodie ?
sections.translation: What is he about ? Or where is this to end at last ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.626b
sections.translation: He ’ ll have to give satisfaction at law , you say , if he turns her out ? That has been already inquired into : aye , aye , you ’ ll have enough to do , if you engage with him ; he is so eloquent .

sections.label: Section 4.3.630
sections.original: Verum pone esse victum eum : at tandem tamen
sections.translation: But suppose he ’ s beaten ; still , however , it ’ s not his life , but his money that ’ s at stake . After I found that the fellow was influenced by these words , I said : We are now by ourselves here ; come now , what should you like to be given you , money down , to drop this suit with my master ,
